title: Pseudoselago spuria
intro: Pseudoselago spuria, also known as the False Selago, is a flowering plant species in the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Southern Africa, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, respiratory infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoselago spuria include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health ailments.
taxonomy: Pseudoselago spuria is classified as a species within the genus Pseudoselago, which belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the figwort family. This family is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Southern Hemisphere. Pseudoselago spuria is a part of the tribe Scrophularieae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The Scrophularieae tribe is characterized by its complex inflorescences and unique floral structure. Pseudoselago spuria's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
